mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.5 percent signals severe labor market deterioration that depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings forecasts, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in >55% of triggered months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and housing demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Joint positive monthly changes yield negative 20-day forward returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.0 percent month-over-month during a monetary easing cycle signals rapid capacity utilization recovery, generating positive short-term equity momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP contraction exceeding 2.0 percent signals severe aggregate demand destruction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as equity analysts downgrade earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in >55% of triggered quarters, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll employment growth directly correlates with corporate revenue expansion and wage-driven consumption, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns when payrolls increase.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising retail sales and falling GDP indicates inventory drawdown rather than demand collapse, generating positive short-term equity returns as earnings surprises materialize.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances, or the divergence signal shows no statistical edge over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth during a post-shock recovery phase signals capacity utilization rebound, driving positive equity returns as industrial sector earnings improve and supply chains normalize.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered months, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during recovery periods.
